title: unexpected mix / max value from SPARQL query
description:
(Virtuoso version 07.20.3217 on Linux (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu), Single Server Edition)
After uploaded the example data in the graph <http://example.org/graph>:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<rdf:RDF
   xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
   xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
   xmlns:ex="http://example.org/"
>
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://example.org/observation/1">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#Observation"/>
    <ex:indicator rdf:resource="http://example.org/indicator/IND-1"/>
    <ex:value rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">B</ex:value>
  </rdf:Description>
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://example.org/observation/2">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#Observation"/>
    <ex:indicator rdf:resource="http://example.org/indicator/IND-1"/>
    <ex:value rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">B</ex:value>
  </rdf:Description>
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://example.org/observation/3">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#Observation"/>
    <ex:indicator rdf:resource="http://example.org/indicator/IND-1"/>
    <ex:value rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">A</ex:value>
  </rdf:Description>
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://example.org/observation/4">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#Observation"/>
    <ex:indicator rdf:resource="http://example.org/indicator/IND-1"/>
    <ex:value rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">A</ex:value>
  </rdf:Description>
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://example.org/observation/5">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#Observation"/>
    <ex:indicator rdf:resource="http://example.org/indicator/IND-1"/>
    <ex:value rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">C</ex:value>
  </rdf:Description>
  <rdf:Description rdf:about="http://example.org/observation/6">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#Observation"/>
    <ex:indicator rdf:resource="http://example.org/indicator/IND-1"/>
    <ex:value rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string">D</ex:value>
  </rdf:Description>
</rdf:RDF>

It is wanted to obtain the min and max values. So, this SPARQL query is used:
PREFIX ex:<http://example.org/>
SELECT ?indicator (min(?value) as ?minValue) (max(?value) as ?maxValue)
FROM <http://example.org/graph>
WHERE{
?obs ex:indicator ?indicator;
     ex:value ?value .
}

The values expected are:
http://example.org/indicator/IND-1
"A"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string>
"D"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string>

but the result obtained is
http://example.org/indicator/IND-1
"B"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string>
"C"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string>

Any ideas of this behaviour?
key_bug_features: SPARQL query for min and max value extraction
bug_feature_seed: SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (MIN(?v) AS ?minV) (MAX(?v) AS ?maxV) FROM <http://example.org/graph> WHERE { ?s ex:value ?v };

SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?indicator (MIN(?v) AS ?minV) (MAX(?v) AS ?maxV) WHERE { GRAPH <http://example.org/graph> { ?s ex:indicator ?indicator ; ex:value ?v } } GROUP BY ?indicator;
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (MIN(STR(?v)) AS ?minV) (MAX(STR(?v)) AS ?maxV) FROM <http://example.org/graph> WHERE { ?s ex:value ?v };

SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (MIN(?v) AS ?minV) (MAX(?v) AS ?maxV) FROM <http://example.org/graph> WHERE { ?s ex:value ?v FILTER (datatype(?v) = <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string>) };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?indicator ?v FROM <http://example.org/graph> WHERE { ?s ex:indicator ?indicator ; ex:value ?v } ORDER BY ?v;
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?indicator (MIN(?v) AS ?minV) FROM <http://example.org/graph> WHERE { ?s ex:indicator ?indicator ; ex:value ?v } GROUP BY ?indicator;
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?indicator (MAX(?v) AS ?maxV) FROM <http://example.org/graph> WHERE { ?s ex:indicator ?indicator ; ex:value ?v } GROUP BY ?indicator;
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (MIN(?v) AS ?minV) FROM <http://example.org/graph> WHERE { ?s ex:value ?v };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT (MAX(?v) AS ?maxV) FROM <http://example.org/graph> WHERE { ?s ex:value ?v };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT ?indicator (MIN(?v) AS ?minV) (MAX(?v) AS ?maxV) WHERE { ?s ex:indicator ?indicator ; ex:value ?v } GROUP BY ?indicator;
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE observations (id INT PRIMARY KEY, indicator VARCHAR(255), value VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);
INSERT INTO observations (id, indicator, value) VALUES (1, 'http://example.org/indicator/IND-1', 'B');
INSERT INTO observations (id, indicator, value) VALUES (2, 'http://example.org/indicator/IND-1', 'A');
INSERT INTO observations (id, indicator, value) VALUES (3, 'http://example.org/indicator/IND-1', 'C');
SELECT indicator, MIN(value) AS minValue, MAX(value) AS maxValue FROM observations GROUP BY indicator;
